mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, it signals a structural labor market recovery that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential investment indicate sustained consumer credit expansion and wealth effects, which jointly drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as construction and related sectors boost earnings.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising industrial production leads equity prices as capacity utilization expands and unit labor costs remain contained, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, indicating that supply expansion has peaked and profit margins are compressing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5 percent monthly rate triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeding 0.5%, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC when tested against actual forward returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market healing, which supports sustained corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the correlation between the combined labor indicator and SPX returns drops below 0.1 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth directly correlates with higher corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting short-term equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive growth, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with elevated policy rates, rising real yields crowd out equity valuations and increase discount rates applied to long-duration assets, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 2.5% and CPIAUCSL rising, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward negative outcomes in this regime.
